heroSection.title: Job Tracker
heroSection.subTitle: React + Express/Node.js + MongoDB
heroSection.description: A Gmail-synced job application tracker that automatically classifies emails into applications, interviews, offers, and rejections using a local AI model with a deterministic regex fallback.

mainContent.overview: Developing a full-stack job application tracker that connects to Gmail via OAuth and streams emails through a hybrid classification pipeline: a deterministic regex parser handles the majority of emails and with a local Ollama LLM (qwen2.5:7b) as fallback for ambiguous cases. Applications surface in a Kanban board and sortable table with live search, CSV import/export, and a stats bar tracking total, active, offers, and interview rate.

keyFeature.body: Streams emails in configurable scan windows (30/60/90/180 days), skipping already-synced message IDs and emitting real-time progress to the UI as each batch is processed

Deterministic regex parser runs first and covers the majority of emails at minimum cost. Only unresolved emails reach the local Ollama LLM, and regex always stays authoritative for company name and role to prevent hallucination

Company matching uses a union of domain and name probes, treats descriptor suffixes (Technologies, Solutions) as the same entity, and uses job numbers as the strongest dedup key to prevent duplicate records
Kanban board with collapsible columns, sortable table with pagination, live search, provenance badges (Imported, Parser, AI), and a stats bar: Total, Active, Offers, Interview Rate

Import applications without connecting Gmail at all, or export to a portable 12-column CSV that opens in Excel or Google Sheets. The export is fully re-importable, so your data stays usable outside the app entirely

walkthrough.subheader: All your applications at a glance
walkthrough.description: Four Kanban columns (Applied, Interview, Offer, Rejected) each collapsible to 3 cards with a Show More expander. A live search bar above the board filters cards by company or role in real time. Cards display company, role, interview stage pill, provenance badges (Parser, AI, Imported), dates, and a notes preview. A stats bar at the top tracks Total, Active, Offers, and Interview Rate. The Interview Rate uses a sticky flag so interviewed-then-rejected applications still count, with a manual override checkbox for edge cases.

An alternative to the board for a denser view of all applications. Sortable columns, 25-row pagination, and the same live search bar shared with the board view. Newly synced or imported rows are highlighted with a green left border. Provenance badges appear inline in the Company column, and edit/delete buttons show on row hover.

Connects via Google OAuth and scans Gmail with a tuned query combining a positive job-phrase group with a large noise-exclusion list. Choose a scan window (30/60/90/180 days) from the header dropdown and hit Sync Gmail. A real-time progress bar and live count update inline next to the button as each batch lands, and a summary line shows exactly what was added or updated once the sync completes. Each synced message ID is stored so re-syncs never reprocess old mail.

walkthrough.subheader: Your data, anywhere, no sync required
walkthrough.description: Export any time to a clean 12-column CSV (Company, Role, Status, Stage, Reached Interview, Date Applied, Last Response, Job URL, Notes, Source, Company Domain, Job ID) that opens directly in Excel or Google Sheets. Import works independently of Gmail sync, so users who prefer not to connect their inbox can still track every application by uploading a CSV manually. The file is also fully re-importable: Company Domain and Job ID are included in the export so any row re-imported later merges onto the correct existing record instead of creating a duplicate. Application data stays portable and readable outside the app entirely.

A clean modal for adding or editing any application manually. Fields include Company, Role, Status, Date Applied, Last Response, Job URL, and Notes. The Interview Stage dropdown appears only when status is set to Interview. For rejected applications, a Reached Interview checkbox is shown to ensure the interview rate stays accurate even after a rejection. User-edited notes are never overwritten by a future sync.
